invariants_list.rule: The PASS command must be preceded by a USER command.
Authentication (USER/PASS sequence resulting in 230) must be completed before issuing LIST, RETR, or STOR.
A data channel establishment command (PASV, PORT, EPSV, or EPRT) must precede data transfer commands (LIST, RETR, STOR, NLST, APPE).
The QUIT command can be sent at any time to terminate the session.
If PASS returns a 530 error, the client should restart the sequence with USER.
RNTO must be preceded by RNFR in a rename operation.
Environment metadata commands (SYST, FEAT) are typically called immediately after successful authentication.

The QUIT command can be sent at any time to terminate the session.
If PASS returns a 530 error, the client should restart the sequence with USER.
RNTO must be preceded by RNFR in a rename operation.
Environment metadata commands (SYST, FEAT) are typically called immediately after successful authentication.

invariants_created: The PASS command must be preceded by a USER command.
Authentication (USER/PASS sequence resulting in 230) must be completed before issuing LIST, RETR, or STOR.
A data channel establishment command (PASV, PORT, EPSV, or EPRT) must precede data transfer commands (LIST, RETR, STOR, NLST, APPE).
The QUIT command can be sent at any time to terminate the session.
If PASS returns a 530 error, the client should restart the sequence with USER.
RNTO must be preceded by RNFR in a rename operation.
Environment metadata commands (SYST, FEAT) are typically called immediately after successful authentication.

status_changes.rationale: Trace shows USER command leading to 331 response, indicating transition to password requirement.
Trace shows PASS command leading to 230 response, indicating login success.
Trace shows 530 error on PASS, allowing retry in the same state or return to USER.
Trace shows 200 response for PORT, establishing the active data connection.
Trace shows 150 response for LIST, signaling the beginning of transfer.
Trace shows 150 response for file retrieval start.
The session returns to AUTHENTICATED control after the transfer finishes (226), and PWD is valid in that state.
QUIT terminates the session from any active state.
REIN restarts the session, leading to a 220 banner similar to the initial state.
Restarting authentication after REIN is standard procedure.
Standard session termination via QUIT.
ACCT provides account info without changing the session state.
Informational commands do not change the session state.
Directory changes stay within the authenticated session.
Directory changes stay within the authenticated session.
Extension for CWD, same state behavior.
Extension for PWD, same state behavior.
Extension for CDUP, same state behavior.
Directory creation stays within the authenticated session.
Extension for MKD, same state behavior.
Directory removal stays within the authenticated session.
Extension for RMD, same state behavior.
File deletion stays within the authenticated session.
Setting transfer type stays within the authenticated session.
Setting transfer mode stays within the authenticated session.
Setting structure type stays within the authenticated session.
SMNT command behavior is local to the session state.
System query stays within the authenticated session.
Feature query stays within the authenticated session.
Help command stays within the authenticated session.
No-op command stays within the authenticated session.
MLST is a control channel listing command that doesn't initiate a data transfer state change.
Informational command stays within session.
Status command stays within session.
EPSV is the IPv6/Generic equivalent to PASV.
EPRT is the IPv6/Generic equivalent to PORT.
MLSD initiates a data transfer listing.
STOR initiates a data transfer to the server.
APPE initiates a data transfer to append to a file.
MLSD starts transfer on the established channel.
APPE starts transfer on the established channel.
The sequence is usually: Command -> 150 (Data Transfer State) -> 226 (Transition back to Authenticated). In a transition graph, this represents the return to the base state.
Similar to LIST, the session returns to AUTHENTICATED after the transfer completes.
Similar to LIST, the session returns to AUTHENTICATED after the transfer completes.
Similar to LIST, the session returns to AUTHENTICATED after the transfer completes.

status_changes.description: The PASS command must be preceded by a USER command.
status_changes.rationale: This is a core requirement of RFC 959 and is consistent with the observed sequence in the trace where USER (331) leads to PASS.

status_changes.description: A data channel establishment command (PASV, PORT, EPSV, or EPRT) must precede data transfer commands (LIST, RETR, STOR, NLST, APPE).
status_changes.rationale: Standard FTP behavior requires a data connection to be defined before transfer; traces show 227 or 200 before 150.

status_changes.description: If PASS returns a 530 error, the client should restart the sequence with USER.
status_changes.rationale: Observed behavior in traces where 530 allows for subsequent USER commands to restart authentication.

status_changes.description: RNTO must be preceded by RNFR in a rename operation.
status_changes.rationale: RFC 959 defines this strict sequence; RNFR returns 350 to indicate a pending command, which is then satisfied by RNTO.

status_changes.description: Environment metadata commands (SYST, FEAT) are typically called immediately after successful authentication.
status_changes.rationale: This is a common client behavior observed in traces but not a protocol constraint.

ordering_rules: The PASS command must be preceded by a USER command.

A data channel establishment command (PASV, PORT, EPSV, or EPRT) must precede data transfer commands (LIST, RETR, STOR, NLST, APPE).
The QUIT command can be sent at any time to terminate the session.
If PASS returns a 530 error, the client should restart the sequence with USER.
RNTO must be preceded by RNFR in a rename operation.
Environment metadata commands (SYST, FEAT) are typically called immediately after successful authentication.
